title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 5
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your manager has asked you to write a report on the pros and cons of allowing employees to work from home two days a week. Evaluate the potential impact on productivity and team morale.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Feasibility Report: Proposed Hybrid Work Model

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to evaluate the implications of introducing a hybrid work model, allowing staff to work remotely for two days per week. The assessment considers both productivity and employee morale.

Impact on Productivity
Contrary to traditional concerns, remote work often leads to a rise in output. Employees report fewer interruptions from colleagues, allowing for 'deep work' on complex tasks. Furthermore, the time saved by avoiding a daily commute often translates into higher energy levels and a more focused approach to the working day. However, it must be noted that for certain junior staff, the lack of immediate physical access to senior mentors could potentially slow down their professional development.

Impact on Team Morale
The proposal has received enthusiastic support from the majority of the workforce. A hybrid model offers a better work-life balance, which is a significant factor in employee retention. The trust shown by management in allowing remote work fosters a sense of loyalty and professionalism. To mitigate any potential feelings of isolation, it is essential that the three days in the office are used for collaborative meetings and social interaction to maintain team cohesion.

Recommendations
I recommend a three-month trial period for this model. During this time, productivity should be monitored through clear, output-based targets. Additionally, I suggest investing in better project management software to ensure that communication remains seamless across the team.

Conclusion
A hybrid model is a forward-thinking move that aligns with modern workplace trends. It is likely to enhance both employee satisfaction and overall efficiency.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You recently attended a professional conference that was poorly organised. Write a letter to the organisers expressing your disappointment, mentioning the poor quality of the speakers and the lack of networking opportunities.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Conference Committee,

I am writing to express my profound disappointment with the 'Innovation in Tech' conference held on March 1st. As a regular attendee of your events, I have previously been impressed, but this year's organisation fell far below the expected standard.

My primary concern was the quality of the keynote speakers. While the topics listed in the brochure were promising, the presentations themselves lacked depth and were often repetitive. It felt as though several speakers had not sufficiently prepared, providing only basic information that most professionals in the room already knew. For a premium-priced event, this was highly unsatisfactory.

Furthermore, the schedule allowed almost no time for networking, which is the main reason many of us attend these conferences. The coffee breaks were too short and the lunch area was so overcrowded that it was impossible to hold meaningful conversations with other delegates. The absence of a structured 'meet and greet' session was a significant oversight.

In light of these issues, I feel that the conference did not provide value for money. I would appreciate it if you could inform me of how you plan to improve future events to prevent these problems from recurring. I hope that you will consider offering a partial discount for next year's event as a gesture of goodwill.

I look forward to your response.

Yours faithfully,
Sarah Jenkins

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement on a technology website: 'We want reviews of language-learning apps. Tell us about an app you have used, what its best features are, and whether it is suitable for advanced learners.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
LingoMaster: Is it Enough for Advanced Learners?

In the crowded market of language apps, 'LingoMaster' claims to take users from 'zero to hero'. Having used the app for my Spanish studies over the past year, I have found it to be an excellent tool, though with some caveats for those at the C1 level.

The app's standout feature is its 'Real-World Video' module. Instead of robotic voices, users watch short clips of native speakers in everyday situations—ordering food, debating politics, or telling jokes. This exposure to authentic accents and slang is invaluable for honing listening skills. Additionally, the 'Speech Recognition' feature is remarkably accurate, giving instant feedback on pronunciation nuances that other apps often miss.

However, for advanced learners, LingoMaster has its limitations. While it is fantastic for vocabulary acquisition and listening, the grammar explanations are somewhat oversimplified. A C1 student needs to understand the subtle differences between complex tenses, which the app tends to gloss over. Furthermore, the lack of an option for long-form writing practice means that it cannot be your only study resource if you are preparing for a professional exam.

In conclusion, LingoMaster is a brilliant supplementary tool. Its interactive nature makes daily practice a joy rather than a chore. I would certainly recommend it for maintaining fluency and expanding your vocabulary, but advanced learners should use it alongside a traditional textbook to ensure their grammar remains sharp. It is the best app I have tried, but it works best as part of a broader study plan.
